place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
East Coast area and wanted to share my thoughts on East Coast Park. It remains one of the best-maintained spaces in Singapore, and its extensive connectivity to other parks makes it an ideal destination for long walks or cycling trips. I particularly enjoyed the ability to ride my bicycle all the way to Gardens by the Bay via the park connector; it was always such a calm and refreshing experience.

The park offers a wonderful atmosphere with its blend of lush greenery and sea views. It is exceptionally well-equipped for families and fitness enthusiasts, featuring dedicated exercise stations, children’s play areas, and wide, separate paths for cycling and walking that prevent the space from feeling crowded.

Beyond the scenery, the facilities are excellent. There are convenient bicycle rentals, a variety of dining options in the restaurant area where you can enjoy the sea breeze, and well-maintained BBQ pits and shelters. For those interested in staying longer, the designated camping areas are a great touch, and I’ve always found the washrooms and bathing facilities to be very clean.

Overall, it is a perfect place to relax, exercise, and enjoy nature, whether you are visiting alone or with friends and family.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
East Coast Park is one of the best and most well-maintained parks in Singapore. It is a very big park and is beautifully connected to other parks across Singapore, making it perfect for long walks or cycling trips. The park has lots of nice scenery, with greenery and sea views that make the atmosphere very relaxing. In some areas, there are exercise equipment stations and children’s play areas, which are great for families and fitness lovers.

There is a dedicated cycling path and a separate walking path, both of which are wide and well maintained, so it never feels crowded. You can also rent bicycles, and there are fun activities and attractions that bring in many people. The park has a restaurant area where you can enjoy a variety of foods while feeling the cool sea breeze. You can book BBQ pits, and there are huts available for shelter in case of rain. There are also designated camping areas for those who want to stay overnight.

I used to live near the East Coast area, and what I loved most was riding my bicycle all the way to Gardens by the Bay, as the park connector makes this possible. It was always a calm and refreshing experience. On top of that, the washrooms and bathing areas are very clean and well maintained. Overall, East Coast Park is a perfect place to relax, exercise, eat, and enjoy nature, whether alone, with friends, or with family.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Kallang MRT (EW10) is one of those "old but gold" stations. Not as fancy as the new Downtown Line or Thomson-East Coast Line stations, but very functional and steady.

​The Good Things:
​Convenience: It's an elevated station, so the breeze is quite shiok when waiting for train. If you going to Stadium for concert or football, this is the "secret" alternative. Got one long sheltered walkway all the way to Sports Hub, no need to worry about rain or sun.

​Food & Amenities: Outside the station got many things. If hungry, just walk a bit to Geylang Lorong 1—plenty of good food like the famous beef kway teow or frog porridge. Inside the station also got Mr. Bean and Old Chang Kee for quick bite.

​Accessibility: Right next to the Lorong 1 Geylang Bus Terminal. Very easy to switch from MRT to bus if you heading to Upper Boon Keng or Geylang area.

​The "Can Improve" Part:
​Crowd: Wah, if got Jay Chou or Taylor Swift concert, please be prepared. The station will be super packed. Even though got the bridge, the queue to enter the gantries can be quite scary.
​Vibe: The station looks a bit "uncle" already lah, standard 80s/90s design. Everything clean but just looks slightly dated compared to the newer lines.

​Verdict: Overall, a very reliable station. Clean, well-maintained, and connects you to some of the best food spots in SG. Just avoid the peak hour "concert crowd" can already.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Decent spot for variety, but not for those craving familiar Pakistani flavours
Visited Lau Pa Sat (Lay Pa Sat) recently in Singapore. It’s a lively and iconic hawker centre with plenty of options and a nice atmosphere, especially in the evening when the streets are closed off for dining.
We tried the halal stall and ordered chicken rice noodles, chicken basil vegetables, and tea tree something. While the portions were generous and the service was quick, the overall taste was quite different from what we expected. Everything had a strong seafood-like flavour profile (even the non-seafood items), which didn’t really appeal to us as I can’t stand sea food smell. It felt quite far from Pakistani taste preferences – the spices, seasoning, and balance were very different.
If you’re in the mood to explore local Singaporean or Southeast Asian hawker food and try new things, Lau Pa Sat is a great place with lots of variety. However, if you’re particular about your food and want something closer to Pakistani cuisine, this might not be the best choice.
Cleanliness was just fine for a busy hawker centre, and the pricing was reasonable. Good for tourists or casual explorers, but we probably won’t rush back.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: fantastic place to grab some food and/or beer. Famous for the skewers on the street, especially the chicken satay. Plenty of options to choose from, Indian, Arabic, Chinese, Singaporean, etc. There's something for everyone. Good ventilation and plenty of tables but the place is always packed and noisy (it doesn't take away the enjoyment). A must to visit.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: There's no need to write much about this place; you simply must stop in for a fantastic meal. There are plenty of street food joints offering a variety of Asian cuisines. Everything is perfectly seasoned, with plenty to choose from, so everyone should easily find something to their liking. And the prices are very affordable by Singaporean standards.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Lau Pa Sat: A must-visit for the lively night vibe!

I visited at 9 PM on a Friday, and it was absolutely crowded! The energy outside on Satay Street was incredible, with everyone enjoying grilled food in the open air. Inside the historic building, there are plenty of food stalls to choose from.

The Drinks:
One thing to watch out for is that there are very few drink stalls, so the lines are very long. I bought an Iced Mango Peach ($2.80), but I wouldn't recommend it. It was extremely sweet and didn't contain any fresh fruit—it tasted mostly like syrup. I suggest skipping the "juices" here if you want something fresh.

The Food & Service:
I tried the Thunder Tea Rice ($6.20). It was okay, though it didn't quite fit my personal taste. After eating, the service is efficient; you can either clear your own tray or wait for the staff who come by frequently to collect them.

The Verdict:
It is very crowded and noisy with loud music, but it’s a "must-visit" to experience the local Singaporean vibe. Just be prepared for the crowds!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Tried Na Na Curry at Lau Pa Sat because saw that they are awarded Michelin Bib Gourmand 10 years in a row!

Their chicken curry is rich, slightly creamy, and super comforting. The spices are well-balanced, not too heavy but still very satisfying.

The chicken is tender and soaks up the curry nicely, making every bite flavourful. Pairs really well with both the rice and roti prata!

review_text:

It's also a cool hawker center to check out because it wasn't touristy at all. I was the only obvious tourist I saw there but I got there about an hour before it closed so maybe it's different earlier in the day, I dunno.

review_text: One of the oldest markets in Singapore. Now, very nicely renovated. It has two sections: one is the agri-food market, the other is a hawker center where you can eat cheaply and well.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Kallang Riverside Park is quiet, open, and comforting in a way that feels unforced. The long walking paths beside the river, the soft breeze, and the wide sky create a calm space where everything slows down. It’s not flashy or crowded—just peaceful, steady, and grounding.
This place feels perfect for solo walks, clearing your head, or listening to music while watching the water move. At sunset, the sky turns gentle and warm; at night, the lights reflect softly on the river, making it feel intimate and reflective. It’s the kind of place you go to when you don’t need noise—just space.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: I came here because there were some people said that we could see the otters running around this area and I also saw the sign that otter might cross by the pedestrian way. However, the lucky is not mine that day as I didn't see it coming, but I saw a turtle swimming under the bridge and there were groups of fish swam in the middle of the river. Really enjoy the long walk around this riverside park.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Kallang Riverside Park is a peaceful and scenic riverside park that offers a great escape from the busy city atmosphere of Singapore. The park is well known for its beautiful waterfront views, clean environment, and relaxing walking and cycling paths along the Kallang River.

It is a popular spot for jogging, cycling, photography, and evening walks, especially during sunset when the skyline views become very impressive. The park is also connected to Singapore’s Park Connector Network, making it convenient for long-distance cycling or casual rides around the Marina Bay area.

Visitors can enjoy the calm atmosphere, fresh air, and open spaces while watching boats pass by the river. The location is suitable for families, couples, solo travelers, and fitness enthusiasts. There are also seating areas and resting spots that make it comfortable for relaxing after exercise or sightseeing.

The park becomes especially beautiful at night with the surrounding city lights reflecting on the water, creating a very peaceful urban scenery. Overall, Kallang Riverside Park is highly recommended for anyone looking for a relaxing outdoor experience, cycling route, or scenic riverside walk in Singapore.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
MacPherson MRT is very convenient and nice station. I use it almost everyday and never had any problem. It’s connected to both Circle Line and Downtown Line so changing train is fast and not confusing. Signs are easy to follow.

Station is clean and got lift and escalator, so good for old people or if carry heavy bag. Around the station got many shops and food places also, very useful. Overall it’s a good station and I like it very much.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
I liked the colour blocks theme in Downtown line.

They are bright and looking great and brings rich ambience to the station.

Rest of the facilities are like any other interchange, but no covered walk way to bus stops. Especially exit B bus stop.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Passed by here for interchanging. The MRT station is immaculate, as expected. Toilets have also been at the same clean standards.

Not really a lot of people, and it was very accessible to other MRT lines from here, as well as the paths to interchanging. Tranquil atmosphere too! Wonderful MRT.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: Great infrastructure! I seldom use blue line but it's definitely a good route from bukit panjang all the way to Sungei bedok.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: National Library / Lee Kong Chian Reference Library
review_text:
Visited the National Library Building mainly for The Albatross File: Singapore’s Independence Declassified exhibition, and it turned out to be a meaningful experience. The exhibition itself is relatively small, but the historical significance is immense. It provides a fascinating and candid look into the events leading up to Singapore’s separation from Malaysia in 1965, including documents and notes showing that both sides had, by then, come to see separation as the likely path forward. Seeing the actual letters, transcripts, and handwritten notes made this pivotal chapter of Singapore’s history feel very real and personal

I also took some time to roam around the Lee Kong Chian Reference Library and was impressed by how incredibly well managed the entire place is. The building itself is architecturally striking, modern, spacious, and filled with natural light. The library environment feels calm, organized, and conducive for learning and research, with clear signage and well-maintained facilities throughout

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: National Library / Lee Kong Chian Reference Library
review_text:
2026 January - Did quick look around, enjoyed reading the stories from the mini exhibition Knots of Time. Looks like there are other exhibitions and all sorts of collection, will be back to explore and read.

Great ambience, nice for researchers and the public alike. Place has plenty of space if you'd like to get some work done, offers brilliant views, and even has a skygarden. Power and water is readily available, and theres even sufficient space for a stroll if you're feeling a mental block coming on.

Will be back to check out the albatross files. The atrium at the bottom is very pleasant, and the vaguely 'heritage' old chang kee seems like a good place to check out as well.

place_name: National Library / Lee Kong Chian Reference Library
review_text:
Albatross exhibition of Singapore

Thankful for this curated history that bares all that Singaporeans may not know. We had the privilege of having seniors in their 70s who came along, cementing our understanding of what they had gone thru.

The whole setup allowed us to go at our own pace and the voice of our late and retired ministers add a sense of urgency and realism.

A suggestion for those who are not English educated, as the ministers are speaking in English, please allow translator set made avail in various language to our non English speaking seniors.

Lastly the wooden board is super heavy. Made it very difficult for us to lift up the board to read the files.  Lastly is there a need to have the board hidden? Most don’t know it exist until someone lifted it up.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Aljunied MRT Station (EW9) is a convenient stop on Singapore’s East-West Line, offering easy access to both residential and commercial areas. The station is clean, well-maintained, and efficiently managed, like most MRT stations in Singapore. Its location makes it a gateway to nearby attractions, local eateries, and budget accommodations.

The surrounding area is vibrant, with a mix of traditional shops and modern amenities, providing a glimpse into local life. Connectivity is excellent, with frequent train services that make it easy to travel across the city. Overall, Aljunied MRT is a reliable and practical transit hub for both locals and visitors.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Bustling MRT station in one of the most interesting district of Singapore, Geylang. The station is very spacious with car parks, bike park, surrounding with shop houses and restaurants.

We stayed in Geylang on our last visit and it is very convenience to as the Station is just a few hundred meters from our hotel, and it’s the same line back-and-forth to the Changi Airport. Love the place, and whenever we alight from the MRT, the station makes us feels like home.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: Neat and small (2exits) above ground MRT station closest to Geylang. This is the last one before Paya Lebar interchange and second above ground station coming from the city. Plenty good eateries around with ample parking space in front of the station. Lot of bike rack space as well. Only minus is that as it is above above ground, the platform is open space and there is no AC so it can get hot at times, but luckily the trains are pretty frequent.

review_text:
Quiet CC near Kallang MRT.

As you all know, Community Clobs are now operating from 10am-6pm, daily. Those void decks are operating from2-6pm instead.. Well, this was converted to the current timing after Covid.

Lets just focus on this venue instead. The most favourable aspect of this place is that this, is its location.

Lets dive in!

Location:
Beside hdb residential and industrial areas. There are lots of makan areas as well.

Carpark:
Has limited capark lots. They also have catered for electric charging for EV vehicles. Carpark is open for public to use with charges applicable. Jz fyi, Plenty of carpark around the areas as well.

Amenities:
CC has basketball court, cool rooftop and a very wide frontage. There is a childcare, gym, hall and more. Quite packed with tenants but possible to add on more to liven the place further. Definitely a venue to serve the residents.

All in all, the average CC that one would expect which has lots of areas for events. Attached are our photos from the back view since everyone can see the front ,,🥳

Cheers!

review_text:
Even though this library is not as big as Woodlands and Jurong East Regional Libraries, it still carries plenty of books and it’s spacious enough too with plenty of comfortable seats.
I also like the more relaxed vibe here with plenty of plants.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
My rating: 4/5
What I like in libraries: Books that are available that I originally have on my online To-Be-Read (TBR). Spacious environments. Multiple book genres.

I wish that the Geylang East Library was closer to where I stayed so I could go there more often. The library has a nice location because it’s just 5 minutes away from the MRT. The library has 2 levels. On the first level there is the borrowing books section, and right across the hall there is a children books section. The children’s section is one of the best children’s sections I’ve seen. When I had walked in I noticed immediately that the room was not tiny. The children’s section is a good size for children because some children are very hyper, so if they want to find books that they are interested in they can just keep walking around because the room is a very nice size, and find books. The second level has Cooking, Singapore Collection, Fiction, Non-fiction, Business, and YP (Young People) books. I like it when libraries have books on display so I can maybe browse through them and that’s what the Geylang library had. There’s also this nice long sofa that multiple people can sit on and also a nice place to use a computer on. There are also a few seats and smaller tables in the middle close to the sofa and that’s really nice for having a pile of books and just reading for the day. Overall, I think the Geylang East library is a very nice place to study, use your computer, and read for the whole day if you’re up for it.

review_text: Singapore Swimming Club has a great selection of Indian food and have always enjoyed the food, especially someone who is very discerning. Having said that, off late the quality of paneer dishes seems to be off. The paneer tikka is no longer soft, creamy and flavourful. I think they may have changed their supplier or switched to using frozen paneer. The management should look into it and bring back the original quality paneer.

We recently visited Paya Lebar, and it is quickly becoming one of the most convenient and well-developed areas in Singapore for both business and leisure travelers.

The area features modern developments like PLQ Mall and SingPost Centre, offering a great mix of shopping, dining, and everyday essentials. It is clean, well-planned, and less crowded compared to central tourist areas, making it a comfortable location for visitors.

One of the strongest advantages of Paya Lebar is its excellent transport connectivity via Paya Lebar MRT Station, which connects both the East-West and Circle Lines. This makes it extremely easy for travelers to move across Singapore using MRT, taxis, or private transport services.

At HolidaySG, we often assist guests staying in the Paya Lebar area with Singapore attraction tickets and transport services, including city tours, private transfers, and ferry bookings. Its central connectivity allows travelers to easily plan visits to major attractions such as Marina Bay Sands and Gardens by the Bay without any hassle.

We have also coordinated with guests in this area for tours and ticket bookings, ensuring smooth pickup arrangements and seamless travel experiences across Singapore.

Overall, Paya Lebar is a smart and convenient choice for travelers looking for strong connectivity, modern facilities, and easy access to tickets, tours, and transport services in Singapore.

— Team HolidaySG
Singapore Licensed Travel Agency

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: Singapore’s MRT stations are exceptionally clean, efficient, and well-organized, reflecting the city’s commitment to seamless public transport. The signage is clear, and announcements are made in multiple languages, making navigation easy for locals and tourists alike. Most stations are air-conditioned and equipped with essential amenities such as escalators, elevators, and ticketing machines. Some central stations also feature retail shops and food stalls, adding convenience for commuters. However, peak hours can get crowded, so traveling during off-peak times is recommended for a more comfortable experience.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: Paya Lebar MRT Station is a major interchange in Singapore connecting the **East-West (EW) and Circle (CC) lines**, making it a convenient hub for commuters traveling across the city. The station is modern, spacious, and well-signposted, allowing smooth transfers between lines. Platforms are wide and clean, with ample seating and clear train arrival information. Accessibility is thoughtfully designed, with lifts, escalators, and barrier-free access for wheelchair users and families with strollers. The station is integrated with **Paya Lebar Quarter (PLQ)**, offering direct access to shopping, dining, and office spaces, which adds convenience for both daily commuters and visitors. Peak hours can be crowded, but train frequency helps manage passenger flow efficiently. Overall, Paya Lebar MRT Station is a clean, well-maintained, and highly functional interchange that plays an important role in Singapore’s MRT network, combining connectivity with practical amenities.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: I visited here for a morning walk. The park is quite large, so you'll definitely work up a sweat walking around. I highly recommend bringing a water bottle. It's full of nature, and spotting a wild chicken was a fun experience.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Relaxing spot, perfect for a nature retreat while in the city. A lot less crowded than you'd expect, considering it's central spot.

It's a collection of spots really, rather than a single destination, with lifestyle dining, museums, educational spice gardens, wedding photo destinations, or just a plain picnic spot. It's much less of a climb than you'd think, is great all around.

View from the top is a comforting one, nice to see the cityscape.

One interesting thing is how it's occupied mainly by maids, foreign workers, and tourists. As an aside, Chinese tourists and their themed outfits are kinda fun! Maybe we should look into that. Under-utilized by Singaporeans - thats your tax dollars at work you know, might as well use it.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: This is a quiet green park in Singapore. Looks like an old park with lot of greenery. It's clam, quiet and with lot of shade its ideal for a relaxing time. The famous Fort canning tree tunnel is also located here. Entrance is free. Popular place amongst the tourists. Good place to relax.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Fort Canning Park is a lush green destination right in the heart of the city, notable for its tranquil atmosphere and historical significance. It's perfect for strolling, relaxing, and taking photos, especially with its famous "tree tunnel" viewpoint.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Visited in the late morning before the weather got too hot , historical park with heritage buildings & a gallery of history of this place was event fully refreshing .

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Old Airport Road Food Centre exceeded my expectations with a great variety of Singapore heritage dishes. I also loved the creativity behind many stall names referencing places like Chong Pang, Kallang, and Blanco Court — adds local charm.

• Old Airport Road Fried Hokkien Mee (#01-99) – 4/5: Wet-style Hokkien Mee with decent wok hei, generous prawns, good value.

• Famous Old Airport Fried Oyster (#01-101) – 3/5: Soggy omelette, overcooked prawns, not worth $10.

• Chong Pang Huat BBQ (#01-90) – 3/5: Expensive, small portions, hygiene issues.

• Jin Fried Rice (#01-63) – 5/5: Fragrant mala fried rice with pork chop — excellent, must-try.

• Chuan Loo Bee Hokkien Food (#01-111) – 2/5: Tough pork ribs, odd sauce, disappointing.

• Whampoa Soya Bean (#01-68) – 4/5: Solid traditional desserts; soya drinks slightly diluted.

• Unkai Japanese Cuisine (#01-88) – 5/5: Generous salmon bowl, wallet-friendly, satisfying.

• Western Barbeque (#01-53) – 3/5: Chicken chop okay but lacks flavour; queues are long.

• Famous Kallang Airport Fried Oyster (#01-55) – 2/5: Oily, overwhelming, still hunting for a better version.

• Salt (#01-128) – 4/5: Aglio Olio with teriyaki chicken — decent but slightly pricey.

• Yi Ji Fried Prawn Noodles (#01-73) – 3.5/5: Carrot cake decent, noodles not outstanding.

• Xin Mei Xiang Lor Mee (#01-116) – 4/5: Double Fish Lor Mee decent; broth mediocre, some service issues.

• Heritage Tanglin Puff – 5/5: Singapore institution since 1952. Freshly fried puffs with generous fillings — curry potato and curry chicken are standouts. 6 for $12, great value.

• Ah Yee Hong Kong Roasted (#01-77) – 4/5: Three-meat combo rice ($10) — char siew well caramelised, roast pork had satisfying crackling, but roast duck was chewy and rice was dry. Chilli a nice touch. Would love a chicken option on the menu. Worth a visit for the char siew and roast pork.

Overall, a must-visit for variety and heritage flavours, though some stalls are hit-or-miss. Will keep exploring and updating!​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: Old Airport Road Food Center is one of popular places for breakfast and lunch.  There are many nice food around such as Lor Mee, Hokkien Mee, Prawn Noddle, Chicken Rice, Roasted Pork and Duck, Western Food, Wanton Noddle.  There is also a new NTUC at level 2.  This food Center can be quite packed during peaked hours.  If you are around Kallang area, I will recommend you guys to drop by …

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Great hawker centre but it can get a little stuffy. Try to grab one of the round tables by the entrances or near the ventilation areas for more space and cool air.

Loved the lor mee! Char koay teow and prawn mee we’re decent too.

If you’re getting ais kacang or cendol, get it from the popular stall in the middle to avoid disappointment!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Very nice and patient uncle who runs this stall. I always thought sugar canes all tasted the same, until I tried this. Happened to find a seat in front of this stall and decided to just buy from him. The sugar cane was really fresh and had a clean taste (there was not that sugary aftertaste). Uncle was also telling me that his is not soaked in sugar syrup and yet it was still naturally sweet! Highly recommended!

I think $2 for a cup, extra for lemon.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: One of my favourite food centres. Great selection and food. Easy to get to via Bus 36 from Changi. Due for a nice power cleaning though.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Refurbished recently. Some nice deco outside but not much has changed.
The net playground is still there, which is a good thing and a unique attraction to this mall.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
🟠 City Square Mall
180 Kitchener Road
Singapore 208539

🟣 Location =  🟣 the mall is located at the junction of Serangoon Road and Kitchener Road.

🟣 It is directly connected to the Farrer Park MRT station via B1 level passage-way.
______________________________________________
⬛️  Historical Context: The mall actually sits on the former site of the New World Amusement Park, which was a famous landmark in the Jalan Besar area for decades.

⬛️  When I was a child in the early 1970s, my late Peranakan father used to bring me to New World to jalan jalan.
________________________________________________
🟠 The mall had undergone a SGD50 million massive revamp from September 2023 to early 2026.

🟠  The revamp added 26,000 sq ft of space, featuring a new 24,000 sq ft "Gastro Square" Level 4 dining area, new playgrounds, and classy luxurious interior upgrades.

🟨 The perimeters ( outside stretch of the mall)
*************************************************
🟨  There is a small verdant green park at the back of the mall, where a decomissioned MRT train cabin sits. ▪︎It is a hub for educational purposes.

🟨 There is also a small Merry-Go-Round & a Tea Cup Spinning Ride, reminiscent of the rides I used to enjoy in the 1970s.

🟩 For the interior of the mall itself
************************************
➡️ Below Info ➡️  is from the lift directory panel.

🟩L1 = City Green

🟩 B1 = Underpass to MRT, Taxi Stand, Pick-Up & Drop-Off, Green Square/ NTUC Fairprice/ Q&M Medical & Dental

🟩 B2=  ATM / Customer Service Counter/ Don Donki

🟩 B3=  Car Park + Food Square + Food Republic

🟩 B4= Car Park

⬛️ L9 To L6 = Schools & Offices  + Singapore Sustainability Academy (SSA)

⬛️ L5 = Discovery Square & Golden Village Cinema

⬛️ L4=  Gastro Square & CONNECT @ City Square Mall ( 🔴5 social service agencies)

⬛️ L 3 =  MST Golf Super Store

⬛️ L2 =  Decathlon & Uniqlo
________________________________________
🔴 Important info:
*******************
🔴 Level 4 features a CONNECT at City Square Mall, a dedicated community space comprising 5 ( five) social service agencies, affiliated with NCSS ( ▪︎National Council of Social Services), which itself is a statutory board under MSF.

🔴 MSF = Ministry of Social and Family Development.

🔴 The five agencies on Level 4 are
**************************************
● Counselling and Care Centre
● Kampong Kapor Community Services
● Singapore Children’s Society
● The Salvation Army

● Eagles Mediation & Counselling Centre ➡️ at B2 level

➖️ Dated 24 March 2026/ Tuesday.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
If you are around Little India area, City Square Mall is actually a pretty nice place to chill and makan.
It's just a short walk from Mustafa Centre and the mall has a good mix of food spots, from quick bites to proper restaurants and food court options. Halal food choices are quite easy to find too.

Shopping wise, it's the kind of mall that feels practical and comfortable. Got everyday stores like Uniqlo and Decathlon, so you can actually shop without everything feeling overly expensive.

Bonus point for tea lovers… theres Beutea too🧋

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
It’s a nice mall and came here because to buy a drink from the nice supermarket. There is a Rest n Go area where there are many massage chairs. You have to scan the QR code to make the payment first and select which package. It’s worth as paying for $15 and 70mins of relaxation! Even paying $10 and 45mins of relaxation is good too. There were many willing to pay and relax on the chairs here and that’s good.

Latest update! A wonderful discovery and if you’re looking for a barber nearby this mall, there’s one! Jerudili Barber and it’s operated by a passionate with professionalism guy. He takes care of your hair well and really comfortable. He cuts hair great too! Please do support since it’s quite new. It’s at 681 Race Course Road and exit F, once alighted from Farrer Park MRT station. I have attached the barber shop photo too which includes the address.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
It’s a very big mall with many renowned international brands like Levi’s, Breadtalk, and toy stores for the little ones like ToysRUs etc. Also sometimes there is a flea market inside the mall with some cool toys and books for a great price. Lots of restaurants as well in easy access.

Outside there is a musical park where you can play some soothing music or relax on the hammocks and there is also a eco bus, with environmental activities and it also spreads awareness about the environment. In all, it’s a great mall!

If you are around Little India area, City Square Mall is actually a pretty nice place to chill and makan.
It's just a short walk from Mustafa Centre and the mall has a good mix of food spots, from quick bites to proper restaurants and food court options. Halal food choices are quite easy to find too.

Shopping wise, it's the kind of mall that feels practical and comfortable. Got everyday stores like Uniqlo and Decathlon, so you can actually shop without everything feeling overly expensive.

Bonus point for tea lovers… theres Beutea too🧋

It’s a nice mall and came here because to buy a drink from the nice supermarket. There is a Rest n Go area where there are many massage chairs. You have to scan the QR code to make the payment first and select which package. It’s worth as paying for $15 and 70mins of relaxation! Even paying $10 and 45mins of relaxation is good too. It’s at 681 Race Course Road and exit F, once alighted from Farrer Park MRT station. I have attached the barber shop photo too which includes the address.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Woodlands Waterfront Park is a relaxing and scenic park located in the northern part of Singapore. It faces the Straits of Johor, so visitors can actually see Malaysia across the water. The park covers around 11 hectares and has a 1.5-kilometre waterfront promenade, which makes it a great place for walking, jogging, or cycling while enjoying the sea breeze.
One of the most interesting features of the park is the 400-metre Woodlands Waterfront Jetty, which is the northernmost point of mainland Singapore. Walking along the jetty is a really nice experience because you get a wide view of the sea and the coastline. Many people go there to fish, take photos, or just relax while looking at the view. In the evening, the sunset from the jetty is especially beautiful and creates a calm atmosphere.
In my opinion, the park is a great place to visit if you want to take a break from the busy city. The long paths are perfect for a peaceful walk, and the cool sea breeze makes the environment very comfortable. There are also open spaces where families can have picnics, as well as a playground where children can play and climb.
Another thing I like about the park is that it is usually less crowded compared to many other parks in Singapore. Because of this, it feels quieter and more relaxing. Overall, Woodlands Waterfront Park is a wonderful place to enjoy nature, spend time with family and friends, and appreciate the beautiful waterfront scenery.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
This seaside park has an interesting history. The jetty, which is the centerpiece of the park, was a mooring location for the Malayan/Malaysian Navy when Singapore was still within the federation. It's surprising that the plot of land was still in the hand of Malaysian government till it's handed over in 1980s (almost 20 years upon the secession). The bollards at the jetty point out to the place's not-so-distant past.

Since the construction of the RTS train, it has been pretty popular place for the locals to take selfie, with the backdrop of the viaduct. Funnily enough, in spite of advisory against fishing at the jetty (posted all over), seems this is just a live and let live policy lol.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Bugis Street is one of the best places to visit in Singapore for shopping, street food, and local vibes. The area is always lively and full of energy, with many affordable fashion items, souvenirs, snacks, and accessories to explore. Really suitable for tourists or anyone who enjoys walking around and discovering unique shops.

There are also many food stalls and nearby cafés around Bugis, making it a great place to relax after shopping. The atmosphere becomes even more interesting at night with the bright lights and busy crowd. Convenient location, easy to access by MRT, and a nice place to experience the modern and local side of Singapore at the same time. Highly recommended for shopping lovers and first-time visitors to Singapore.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
I absolutely love this traditional market. It sells all kinds of goods, modern and traditional items, food and drinks, souvenirs, and various trinkets.

There's also a large fruit stall outside. If you're staying at a nearby hotel and craving fruit, this is definitely an excellent choice, incredible value and extremely fresh.

The food options here are overwhelming. The best way is to eat while you walk, and you can definitely eat until you're full. After you're full, there are still all kinds of desserts and drinks to enjoy.

This place can easily rival traditional markets and night markets from around the world. It's highly recommended for a visit!

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
The street is always bustling with energy, filled with countless small shops and stalls offering trendy clothes, accessories, souvenirs, and much more at affordable prices. It’s a great place for budget shopping and picking up unique items.
Even if you’re not planning to shop a lot, just walking through the market is an experience in itself. There are also plenty of food stalls and drink options around, so you can enjoy local snacks and refreshing juices while exploring.
It can get quite crowded, especially in the evenings, so be prepared for that. But overall, it’s a must-visit place in Singapore for shopping lovers and anyone who enjoys vibrant street markets!

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: We went to check on the souvenir prices and among all souvenir places we’ve been, we think bugis street has the best prices already. Super affordable and not so much crowd. You can haggle a little and have some food and snacks that you can try around while shopping.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Bugis Street is a lively and bustling shopping spot that a lot of visitors recommend for a fun and budget-friendly experience. Many people love how wide and varied the shops are, with everything from affordable fashion and accessories to quirky souvenirs, making it a great place to explore and shop without spending too much.
The street food stalls and snack options add to the vibrant atmosphere — you can easily spend some time tasting local bites while browsing through the stalls. It does get crowded, especially in the evenings and on weekends, but that’s part of the energetic vibe here.
Overall, it’s a great place for casual shopping, good street food, and vibrant street life — 4 stars ⭐⭐⭐⭐.

Bugis Street ek bahut lively aur bustling shopping destination hai jise kaafi log budget-friendly shopping ke liye recommend karte hain. Yahan shops ka variety wide hai — saste fashion, accessories, souvenirs aur unique items milte hain, jis se shopping explore karna fun ho jata hai.
Street food stalls aur local snacks bhi yahan ka vibe aur enhance karte hain — shopping ke beech mein local bites try karna easy aur enjoyable hota hai. Thoda crowd hota hai, especially evenings aur weekends par, lekin yeh Bugis Street ka energetic charm hi hai.
Overall, casual shopping, good street food aur vibrant atmosphere ke liye ek badiya place — 4 stars ⭐⭐⭐⭐.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
Potong Pasir Community Club stands out for its high land-use efficiency, successfully integrating essential services (POSB AutoLobby, 24-hour Sheng Siong, KFC, SingPost) within a community framework. This coexistence creates a complex security perimeter.

​The presence of high-traffic commercial tenants necessitates robust physical security. I noted clear sightlines and effective lighting in the transition zones between the CC facilities (like the unique Archery Academy) and the commercial areas, which is a key tenet of CPTED (Crime Prevention Through Environmental Design).

It probably has the cleanest toilets in Potong Pasir neighbourhood. However, it can consider updating directories and signs on the premises. Newspaper reading corner is usually occupied by seniors and multi-purpose hall users. Advisories could be pasted on the tables for fair and considerate usage.

​A highly functional, securely managed community hub that serves as a benchmark for neighborhood-centric integrated design.

review_text: Mustafa Centre is an incredible shopping destination! I always find exactly what I need there, and the variety of items available is truly amazing. 😊 The atmosphere is lively, and it's a great place to explore. So many unique finds! 🛍️ Love it! ✨

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
A 24/7 Shopping Paradise with Great Prices! 🛒🇸🇬
Rating: ⭐⭐⭐⭐ (4/5)
Mustafa Centre is one of the most famous shopping malls in Singapore, and for a good reason. It’s open 24/7, making it incredibly convenient for anyone to shop at any time of the day or night.
Why I recommend it:
• Best Prices: Almost all goods are sold at very reasonable and competitive prices.
• One-Stop Shop: From electronics and jewelry to groceries and souvenirs, you can find everything here. It’s the go-to spot for foreigners and migrant workers to buy gifts and essentials for their families back home before vacation.
Important Tip for Shoppers:
I highly recommend checking the expiry dates on all food and consumable items before you buy. Try to ensure they have at least one year of validity.
My Experience: I’ve had issues multiple times where I bought items in bulk, but since the expiry date was too close, I couldn't finish them in time. This is a big waste of money, so please double-check your basket before heading to the cashier!
#MustafaCentre #SingaporeShopping
#LittleIndia
#TravelTips
#BudgetShopping #SmartShopping

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Mustafa Centre is one of the most unique shopping experiences in Singapore. Located in the heart of Little India, this massive multi-level store literally has everything — from electronics and perfumes to groceries, souvenirs, jewelry, clothing, and even travel essentials.
What makes it even more impressive is that it’s open 24 hours a day. Whether you need something late at night or just enjoy browsing without crowds during off-peak hours, it’s incredibly convenient.
The store can feel a bit overwhelming at first because it’s packed floor-to-ceiling with products and narrow aisles, but that’s part of its charm. Prices are competitive, and you can often find great deals, especially on chocolates, cosmetics, and electronics.
If you’re staying in the Little India area or nearby, it’s definitely worth a visit — even if just to see how massive and diverse the selection is. A true Singapore experience for shoppers.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
A 24/7 Shopping Paradise with Great Prices! 🛒🇸🇬
Rating: ⭐⭐⭐⭐ (4/5)
Mustafa Centre is one of the most famous shopping malls in Singapore, and for a good reason. It’s open 24/7, making it incredibly convenient for anyone to shop at any time of the day or night.
Why I recommend it:
• Best Prices: Almost all goods are sold at very reasonable and competitive prices.
• One-Stop Shop: From electronics and jewelry to groceries and souvenirs, you can find everything here. It’s the go-to spot for foreigners and migrant workers to buy gifts and essentials for their families back home before vacation.
Important Tip for Shoppers:
I highly recommend checking the expiry dates on all food and consumable items before you buy. Try to ensure they have at least one year of validity.
My Experience: I’ve had issues multiple times where I bought items in bulk, but since the expiry date was too close, I couldn't finish them in time. This is a big waste of money, so please double-check your basket before heading to the cashier!
#MustafaCentre #SingaporeShopping
#LittleIndia
#TravelTips
#BudgetShopping #SmartShopping

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Mustafa Centre is one of the most unique shopping experiences in Singapore. Located in the heart of Little India, this massive multi-level store literally has everything — from electronics and perfumes to groceries, souvenirs, jewelry, clothing, and even travel essentials.
What makes it even more impressive is that it’s open 24 hours a day. Whether you need something late at night or just enjoy browsing without crowds during off-peak hours, it’s incredibly convenient.
The store can feel a bit overwhelming at first because it’s packed floor-to-ceiling with products and narrow aisles, but that’s part of its charm. Prices are competitive, and you can often find great deals, especially on chocolates, cosmetics, and electronics.
If you’re staying in the Little India area or nearby, it’s definitely worth a visit — even if just to see how massive and diverse the selection is. A true Singapore experience for shoppers.

review_text:
Both of my kids have been with this preschool since their infant days, and watching how much they have grown over the years truly means a lot to us. The teachers are incredibly warm, caring, and dedicated, and we always felt that they were very well taken care of.

The school provides many opportunities for the children to explore such as through excursions and their isteam program, and during K2, we could clearly see how thoughtfully they prepared the kids for Primary 1 — from daily life skills like managing their belongings and personal hygiene, to strong academic foundations in phonics and Chinese literacy.

We are very grateful for the love, guidance, and effort from the teachers. Highly recommended to any parent looking for a nurturing and well-rounded preschool for their child. 💛

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
Our son joined Cambridge Preschool @ MacPherson at just 10 weeks old and is now moving on to playgroup at 18 months. From day one, the teachers and staff have been nothing short of amazing. They cared for him like their own, celebrated every little milestone, and created a safe and joyful environment where friendships naturally blossomed.

We’ve watched him grow from a tiny infant to a confident, happy toddler, and we couldn’t have asked for a better start to his school journey. The teachers’ dedication, warmth, and professionalism shine through in everything they do.

As we reflect on our time here, we honestly have nothing but gratitude—it’s been a wonderful first school experience that has exceeded every expectation. Thank you to everyone at Cambridge MacPherson for giving our son such a beautiful beginning.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
A Truly Wonderful School Experience
As expats, choosing the right school for our son was one of the most important decisions we had to make—and looking back, it was truly the best decision we could have made. From the very beginning, the school welcomed us warmly and made us feel supported every step of the way.
The care and attention given to our son have been outstanding. It’s clear how professional and dedicated the entire teaching staff is. Special thanks to Teacher Nell and Miss Sharon—their kindness, patience, and genuine care made a world of difference in our son's daily life and in ours as parents. We always felt heard, understood, and truly part of the school community.
The rest of the teachers have also been amazing and played a key role in his learning journey. Each one brought something special to his experience. The environment is not only educational but also incredibly nurturing, with everyone being genuinely sweet and supportive.
We are so grateful to have found this school—it has made a meaningful impact on our family’s life, and especially on our son's growth and happiness. Highly recommended to any family looking for a place where their child will be cared for, challenged, and celebrated.

We don’t post photos only for privacy of the children ! However you can see how impecable is the facility when you go there !

review_text:
My daughter has been with Cambridge @ MacPherson since infant care, and now she’s graduating from K2. Over the years, we’ve had the privilege of witnessing the dedication, warmth, and professionalism of the teachers at this centre.

The educators here are truly wonderful. They genuinely care for each and every student, creating a nurturing and safe environment where children can learn, grow, and thrive. Their commitment goes far beyond just teaching — they invest their time, energy, and hearts into making sure every child feels supported and valued.

We are deeply grateful for the patience, love, and hard work the teachers have shown throughout the years. Thank you for being such an important part of our daughter’s early learning journey.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
We would strongly recommend Cambridge @ MacPherson to friends and family!

Our kids started at N1 and N2 in 2023, and our older one will be graduating with the K2 2025 class. So glad that our 2 kids enjoy the school bus rides and going to school at Cambridge!

The main strengths we see are:
(1) high school hygiene (our kids fall sick way less here),
(2) consistent staff retention (both our kids’ main teacher have been with the school for a number of years, and our kids love the teachers and staff. Pretty cosy and stable environment!),
(3) very strong curriculum (lots of STEAM activities, and even optional exposure to robotics competition, wushu competition, overseas cultural trip).

Physical play is also important to us. We see that the school’s indoor playground is a hot fav, and there are lots of nature walks and regular school outings.

We are forever grateful to Teacher Lynee, Guan laoshi, Lin laoshi, Teacher Nell and Ms Sharon for watching our young ones and guiding them in a warm and loving manner 🥲! You have been so important in helping us bring up our children!

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Love this lib. Looks so new and design so contemporary. As with all the new libraries, the design is thoughtful with good reading areas, working areas, charging points. These days I love going to the library because it is such an enjoyment experience. Nestled within heartbeat@bedok with supermarket and f&b. Building has its own car park. Water dispenser avail just outside lib. Lib spread two floors with internal staircase.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
I was surprised to find tourist reviews for such a niche place! This must be the kind of spot people visit only if they’re on an extended trip to Singapore and have the time to explore a community library with no real scenic appeal.

The library is located on the 2nd and 3rd floors of the community center (CC). Taking the escalator is much faster since the cylindrical lift is extremely inefficient—you might have to wait up to 5 minutes for a ride! The upper floors also host other activity facilities, so even on weekdays, there are quite a few people queuing for the lift.

The overall environment here is definitely not as quiet as a university library. You’ll encounter a mix of people, including uncles whose phones aren’t on silent mode and those who talk loudly on their phones.

The 3rd floor is even noisier because it houses the children’s reading area. The noise from kids running around often drifts down to the 2nd floor, making it less peaceful as well.

Sofa seating is almost always occupied—even if there’s no one sitting, personal belongings will be placed there to reserve the spot. There are usually available seats in the hard plastic chair area, though.

If you plan to spend a long time studying or reading here, you might want to bring a long-sleeved coat, as the air conditioning is set to 18°C. For residents dealing with noisy construction at home or in public spaces, this library can be a good escape. Although it’s not particularly quiet, it’s much more peaceful compared to the noise pollution in residential buildings or on the streets.

For visitors/tourists looking to take aesthetic photos, this might not be the best choice. The design of the bookshelves here isn’t as visually appealing as those in the Orchard Road Library. However, if you’re out of places to visit and just want to relax, this spot offers a glimpse into the daily lives of locals and their community atmosphere.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
I visited the NLB Bedok library today to use one of the public PCs. While I was using it, the computer froze and I encountered some difficulties. I sought assistance from the staff, Miss Norsiah , who responded immediately and resolved the issue confidently and efficiently.
I would like to take this opportunity to express my sincere appreciation and thank her for her prompt, professional, and excellent assistance. Her helpfulness made my visit a pleasant experience. Once again, many thanks.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Bedok Public Library staffs is extremely kind and willing to help. It is also we'll designed from interior to exterior. I liked  the library's Teens Study and Reading Area,Early Literary Area,Multimedia Stations area and Magazine Reading Area the most I would strongly encourage you to visit this Library if you want to get BookBugs 3rd release cards as I got 35 Buwan,54 Sisik and 57 Liraes here with only 85 times.The BookBugs dispensing machine and stamp booth is at Level 3 of the librarynear the borrowing station (At level 3 main Entrance).It is also very convenient as it is at Heartbeat@Bedok, which is just 10 mins walk from EW5 Bedok station and there is lift service from level 2 to level 3 if you want to go to the early Literacy area (level 3) from the adult area (level 2) .I would like to visit this library again.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Clean and conducive atmosphere for reading and studying. Most of the spots were occupied by students who seem to be camping there , and elderly reading news paper. As a first time visitor, there are few staff to assist and level 2 entrance were closed. An impressive catalog of books (including other foreign languages ones). There seem to be seminar rooms for special program .

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
One of the greatest gifts a school can give a parent is peace of mind, and Little Atlas Preschool in Geylang delivers this daily.
The campus security protocols are robust and professionally handled, but more importantly, there is a palpable culture of "watchful care."
I’ve noticed that, Arrival and dismissal are organized and secure, ensuring every child is where they need to be.
The emotional environment feels safe; the staff fosters a culture of kindness that makes Matteo and Eliza feel protected from the inside out.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: We are so glad to have enrolled our girl into Little Atlas. All the teachers here are very bubbly, caring and takes good care of the kids. The curriculum involves reading, discovering, exploring and thinking skills. These have made my child love reading alot and have bring out the creativity in her. The consistent updates from teachers also helps us alot in understanding how she have been behaving, coping and learning in school.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
We are absolutely thrilled with the care and education our daughter has received at Little Atlas Preschool. From the moment we enrolled her, we felt welcomed by the warm, nurturing staff who are truly dedicated to the growth and development of the children. The teachers are not only knowledgeable but also incredibly patient, kind, and attentive to each child's individual needs.

Our daughter loves going to school every day! She has gained so much confidence, developed important social skills, and is always excited to share what she's learned. The curriculum is engaging, age-appropriate, and encourages creativity, curiosity, and a love for learning. It's clear that the staff take great care in creating a supportive and safe environment where kids can thrive.

We also appreciate the communication from the school. Whether it's through regular updates, parent-teacher conferences, or fun events, we always feel involved and informed about our daughter's progress. Little Atlas Preschool has truly become a second home for her, and we couldn't be happier with the experience.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: After trying a 3 other preschools, we've finally found the right fit with Little Atlas. The teachers are warm and genuinely engaged and care for the kids. We love getting the weekly updates with real candid photos of our toddler fully participating in the activities - no more forced smiles for the camera like some of his previous school. The curriculum seems to be keeping him challenged and interested. Overall, a really positive experience compared to our past preschool struggles.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
A Fantastic Kindergarten Experience!

We couldn’t be happier with our experience at Little Atlas Preschool. From the moment we walked through the doors, it was clear that this is a place where children are nurtured, valued, and encouraged to grow.

The teachers and staff are warm, caring, and highly skilled. They genuinely take the time to understand each child’s unique needs and personalities, creating a supportive and inclusive environment. Our child has not only gained a love for learning but has also grown socially and emotionally thanks to the thoughtful attention and encouragement provided here.

The curriculum is both engaging and well-rounded, blending structured learning with plenty of play-based activities.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
We visited Maxwell Food Centre at around 8:00 AM on a Monday, so it was still relatively quiet with very few queues. Unfortunately, the famous chicken rice stall was closed, which was a bit disappointing. This was actually my second visit, and I still haven't had the chance to try it!

Thankfully, there are plenty of other stalls serving chicken rice, so we simply picked one without a line and it didn't disappoint. In fact, that's the beauty of Maxwell Food Centre. There are so many food options to choose from, and everything we tried was delicious. The drinks were great too, and the prices are very affordable. Those in the pictures are only some of those we found really delicious.

Whether you're craving local favorites or just looking for a good meal, Maxwell Food Centre never disappoints. If you're in Singapore, make sure to stop by for delicious food at budget-friendly prices. Just keep in mind that if you're hoping to try the famous chicken rice, it's worth checking the stall's opening hours before you go!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: There’s a wide variety of food and the laksa was absolutely amazing. It’s cheaper if you pay cash instead of card. The staffs in charge of cleaning the trays were really proactive. We didn’t try the super crowded stalls, but the ones we had were pretty solid in terms of quality. Also no bad odor

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
I was here in the evening time and most of the stalls were closed for the day.
But I am very fortunate of finding a chicken rice shop still open.
Uncle Louis Famous chicken rice. Uncle was very hands on. He takes each customer's order attentively and gives suggestions along the way.
I ordered chicken with soup and veggies. Very tender, fresh veggie sides and hot rice. Yummy! Welcome local dinner on my 1st night in Singapore.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
A step back in time with fantastic food variety at really bargain prices. Only one of the 3 stall rows has high velocity fans for extra cooling. A few obvious winners among the vendors. We had Vietnamese  Tofu and noodles and vegetarian rice paper rolls.
A local character has found a way to always get his favorite seat - Xtra load rap music. He managed to get a whole table under a fan for himself during the lunch peak. There's someone who understands marketing!
A fantastic authentic street food location that has a busy lunch peak but is much quieter otherwise.
The lady selling fresh crushed fruit and sugar can drinks is a standout nearthe China Town exit.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: My favourite eating place in the city by far. So much to choose from, foodie heaven. There's always a good selection of shops open. Can be very busy at time with queues for the most popular places.  Don't be scared to share tables. When finished, put your tray in designated area before leaving.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Large park with many local residents around that area walking and jogging. Seats are available around this park. There's playground for kids as well. There's also a canoe area in a separate building nearby and it's called "Passionwave". There's toilet facilities too. Quite a number of parking space available for those who came from far. Many companies and schools like to hold their events here as the space is spacious and it is convenient to come here for the bus stops are nearby too.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Well maintained park with walking paths around the reservoir. The sandy path by the water takes more effort than the shady paved path running along side. A good 5km round the park. Different gradients on the paved path add to a good workout. Encountered squirrels and a lone otter too.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Beautiful spot for a walk or jog! Bedok Reservoir's lush greenery and picturesque views of the water make it an ideal destination for outdoor enthusiasts. Well-maintained and clean facilities too. It’s always a nice 5 km walk !

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Weekdays is ok. Weekend is a fish market. Screaming infants and kids running around. Badly controlled during weekends due to small place. Avoid Sundays if you need a place to read.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
I would like to commend the NEX Library for its attentiveness and highly proactive staff. It’s quite impressive how quickly they notice patrons—within just 30 minutes of me sitting on one of the couches, I was disturbed not once, but three times.

What stood out most was the level of dedication shown. One staff member even went the extra mile by physically slapping my chair to wake me up, followed by a firm reminder of “don’t sleep,” delivered publicly. It’s rare to see such enthusiasm in ensuring that no one gets too comfortable.

While I understand that seating is meant to be shared, I didn’t realise that briefly resting would warrant such persistent interruptions and physical gestures. It does make me wonder about the purpose of having couches in the library if taking a short rest isn’t acceptable.

Perhaps moving forward, the same level of attentiveness could be paired with a bit more courtesy and respect when approaching patrons. After all, maintaining a welcoming environment is just as important as enforcing rules.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: I really like the Serangoon library because there is a specific section where there are about 15 seats alone (near the entrance) And I think that's very helpful. There also is a children's section for 10-12 year olds and a 6-8 year old section right next to the 10-12 section. They also have comics for people that like to read comics. Close to the entrance. They have this display of books and I've picked a few books from the display that I've liked so that's very nice. There is a fairly large section for Fiction and I am a Fiction fan so that's great for me. There are multiple computers there too. I don't know if it's the best place to study as a student though. But the library's location is really nice because just one level down (after you exit the library) there's a mall. I personally really like the mall because there's a Fair Price Xtra, restaurants, gadget stores and more. I wish there were more isolated seats instead of many at a certain section. The location outside of the library and mall is also nice because there is a bus station and a taxi station. Overall I would say that the Serangoon library is a very nice library to visit.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Visited three times and harassed by staff each time. They have a children’s section but their expectations for toddler behavior are unrealistic. Zero empathy. I sent my family outside just to check out our books in peace. Even then I would still get nagged with unsolicited and irrelevant advice.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Overall experience is good just that at around 4pm onwards, the library will be super crowded. They have programme room which is free seating without the need to book table but from 1pm onwards, all tables are occupied. Should add another programme room. Also the library can be very cold too

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Angela is very patient in explaining the term and conditions with me. As I’m first time in a gym with her cheerfulness make me less anxious. Thank you Edmund for explaining the detail on the package so clearly, very helpful and patient.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
Fantastic 20-session journey – Highly recommend Fitness First Paya Lebar!

I recently completed an awesome 20-session journey at Fitness First Paya Lebar and cannot recommend this gym enough. The equipment is modern and kept in excellent condition, and the locker and shower facilities are good and provide everything you need for a smooth workout transition. My experience from day one was seamless; Mae provided a very professional and thorough briefing during registration, and the reception staff are consistently pleasant and welcoming. A special thank you goes to my personal trainer, Charlotte, who is truly an exceptional physical trainer and an expert in her field. She is incredibly knowledgeable and patient, taking the time to explain the proper mechanics of every machine and ensuring I understood exactly how to use them safely and effectively. Her friendly and encouraging approach made every session highly productive and enjoyable. If you are looking for a supportive environment and expert guidance to reach your fitness goals, I highly recommend.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
Really grateful to have trainers like Nazree at Fitness First. He’s very experienced, knowledgeable and professional, but also friendly and easy to approach. Even when you’re not his client, he’s always happy to give advice and help out.

I used to attend his BOOM class and it was one of my favourite classes — fun, energetic and very technique-focused. Hope he brings BOOM back again!

Now attending his Hyrox class and I’m impressed by how attentive he is. He remembered my right ankle injury and always gives me options for the workout. You can really tell he cares about members’ safety and progress. .

Highly recommend Nazreen to anyone looking for a dedicated and supportive trainer! 💪

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
Came here for my fitness assessment and had a great experience. The gym and lounge area were very clean and well-maintained, and the front desk staff were friendly and welcoming.

Special mention to Charlotte, who attended to me during the assessment. She was patient, friendly, and very well-versed in the exercises and movements. Even after I shared that my upper body strength was weaker, she remained encouraging and supportive throughout the session. Overall, had a really positive experience with her!

review_text:
I’ve been training at Fitness First Paya Lebar for the past two and a half months, and it has been a very positive experience.

A big part of this is thanks to my personal trainer, Charlotte. She is incredibly helpful, patient, and always fully focused during our sessions. She takes the time to ensure I’m using the correct form, explains each exercise clearly, and adjusts the workouts based on my progress and condition.

I really appreciate how attentive and encouraging she is—it makes each session both effective and enjoyable. I’ve definitely seen improvement since I started, and I feel more confident in my workouts.

Overall, I’m very happy with both the club environment and the quality of training. Highly recommended!

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: There is a basketball court there which is in quite good condition. However, the badminton court is bad. Firstly, the floor is slippery and dirty. Net got hole inside and not in good quality. Very noisy in there. Damn stuffy, no circulation of air. Can  you believe it, the court is exposed to the outside, got wind, a big hole in the wall. Basically impossible to play there. constant wind and slippery court. Waste of money sia. You come here you come for the cafe or to play basketball. Just dont play badminton here. Alsi in a very inconvenient location. Except that it is near a very small hawker centre. Not near Macpherson MRT, need to take bus from there to reach here. Overall a bad experience.
